subtopics.name: Observing gender roles in family work
subtopics.summary: Students observe families in their surroundings to identify if there are separate works for boys and girls and note any differences.

subtopics.name: Collecting data on home appliance usage
subtopics.summary: Students visit friends' homes to gather information on home appliances used in their houses.

subtopics.name: Documenting family changes through photographs
subtopics.summary: Students use personal photographs to identify and describe changes in their family over time.

subtopics.name: Visual representation of family members and their work
subtopics.summary: Students create a visual representation of their family members and articulate their respective roles and tasks.

subtopics.name: Empathy and support for new families
subtopics.summary: Students consider how to assist new families in their neighborhood.

subtopics.name: Understanding and appreciating joint family dynamics
subtopics.summary: Students reflect on the positive feelings associated with joint families helping each other.

subtopics.name: Importance of self-reliance and cooperation
subtopics.summary: Students discuss how individual effort and cooperation can alleviate difficulties for others.

subtopics.name: Reflecting on learning outcomes and skills acquired
subtopics.summary: Students review their ability to explain family changes, ask questions, collect data, draw family members, and help new families.

subtopics.name: Observing Games
subtopics.summary: Students observe a picture depicting various games and identify them.

subtopics.name: Discussing Games
subtopics.summary: Students discuss different aspects of games, such as what games they play, with whom, when, and how they feel while playing.

subtopics.name: Introduction to Game Rules
subtopics.summary: Students learn that games have rules and that preparations are needed before playing.

subtopics.name: Kabaddi Game Play
subtopics.summary: Students read a story about a Kabaddi game to understand how it is played and the role of rules.

subtopics.name: Specific Rules of Kabaddi
subtopics.summary: Students learn the detailed rules of Kabaddi, including 'cant', 'bark line', 'middle line', 'end line', 'retirement box', and safety precautions.

subtopics.name: Rules of Other Games
subtopics.summary: Students are encouraged to discuss and write down the rules of other games they know.

subtopics.name: Purpose of Rules
subtopics.summary: Students understand that rules help control games and ensure proper conduct.

subtopics.name: Rules in Daily Life
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the application of rules in real-world scenarios such as traffic, school, and personal safety.

subtopics.name: Road and School Rules
subtopics.summary: Students learn about rules for crossing roads safely and rules followed in schools for discipline and well-being.

subtopics.name: Personal Body Safety Rules
subtopics.summary: Students are introduced to basic personal safety rules, emphasizing the importance of not talking to strangers and speaking only to trustworthy persons.

subtopics.name: Games for Everyone
subtopics.summary: Students learn that both boys and girls can play various games, promoting inclusivity.

subtopics.name: Famous Sportspersons
subtopics.summary: Students identify and learn about well-known Indian athletes and their respective sports.

subtopics.name: Understanding Victory and Defeat
subtopics.summary: Students learn that winning and losing are natural in games and the importance of sportsmanship, fair play, and hard work.

subtopics.name: Benefits of Playing Games
subtopics.summary: Students learn how playing games contributes to physical health, mental well-being, and social development.

subtopics.name: Playing as a Child's Right
subtopics.summary: Students understand that playing games is a fundamental right for children and the importance of allocating time for play.

subtopics.name: Recognizing Achievements in Sports
subtopics.summary: Students learn about how individuals and teams are recognized for their achievements in sports, with examples like the Indian Kabaddi team and Karnam Malleswari.

subtopics.name: Value of Team Spirit and Sportsmanship
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the importance of working together, adjusting with others, and appreciating winners and encouraging losers.

subtopics.name: Key Vocabulary
subtopics.summary: Students learn and review important terms related to games, rules, and sports.

subtopics.name: Information Skills and Projects
subtopics.summary: Students engage in information gathering and analysis by researching famous sportspersons and discussing game demographics.

subtopics.name: Communication through Mapping Skills, Drawing Pictures and Making Models
subtopics.summary: Students develop spatial and communication skills by drawing and explaining the court of an outdoor game.

subtopics.name: Appreciation, Values and Awareness towards Bio-diversity
subtopics.summary: Students reflect on sportsmanship, ethical behavior towards winners and losers, and the importance of play as a children's right.

subtopics.name: Self-Assessment: I can do these
subtopics.summary: Students self-evaluate their ability to explain rules, observe games, collect information, show sportsmanship, and engage in daily play.

subtopics.name: Understanding compassion for animals
subtopics.summary: Students learn that animals are living beings with needs, and it is important to provide them with food, water, and care, especially when they are injured or young.

subtopics.name: Introduction to Biodiversity
subtopics.summary: Students learn the definition of biodiversity, its presence in nature (especially forests), the right of every organism to live, and the interdependence of living organisms on Earth.

subtopics.name: Threats to Biodiversity
subtopics.summary: Students learn about human activities like cutting forests and hunting animals that lead to the destruction of biodiversity, and the impact of pollution and environmental changes on organisms.

subtopics.name: Endangered Species
subtopics.summary: Students are introduced to the concept of endangered species, understanding that many plants, birds, and animals are disappearing due to human actions, and the importance of protecting them.

subtopics.name: Conceptual Understanding
subtopics.summary: Students answer questions to demonstrate their understanding of animal lifestyles, nests, and biodiversity concepts.

subtopics.name: Questioning - Hypotheses
subtopics.summary: Students engage in a scenario-based activity to formulate questions and develop hypotheses related to animal behavior.

subtopics.name: Experiments - Field Observations
subtopics.summary: Students are instructed to observe bird nests and birds eating, and record their observations, promoting scientific inquiry.

subtopics.name: Information Skills, Projects
subtopics.summary: Students undertake activities like collecting pictures for a scrapbook and compiling information about animal shelters, enhancing their research and organizational skills.

subtopics.name: Communication through Mapping Skills, Drawing Pictures and Making Models
subtopics.summary: Students express their understanding through creative activities such as making models of bird nests and drawing birds, and speaking about them.

subtopics.name: Appreciation, Values and Awareness Towards Bio-diversity
subtopics.summary: Students reflect on ethical questions regarding animal welfare and biodiversity protection, and are encouraged to create slogans and discuss actions for protection.

subtopics.name: General Introduction to Plants
subtopics.summary: Students learn that plants vary in size and type, and are introduced to the concept of different plant categories.

subtopics.name: 5.1 Climbers / Creepers, Shrubs and Trees
subtopics.summary: Students learn to differentiate between climbers/creepers, shrubs, and trees based on their stem characteristics and growth habits, with examples.

subtopics.name: Observing Plant Parts
subtopics.summary: Students observe a plant to identify its various parts and discuss which parts are above and below the soil.

subtopics.name: 5.2.1. Roots
subtopics.summary: Students learn about roots, their location below the soil, their function in supporting the plant and absorbing water and nutrients, and observe differences in roots of various plants.

subtopics.name: 5.2.2. Stem
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the stem, its location above the soil, its role in supporting branches, leaves, fruits, and flowers, and its function in transporting water and nutrients. They also observe variations in stem characteristics.

subtopics.name: 5.2.3. Leaves, flowers and fruits.
subtopics.summary: Students learn about leaves, flowers, and fruits as parts of a plant, their functions, and their importance. They also learn about chlorophyll, photosynthesis, and the role of the sun as an energy source.

subtopics.name: Flowers as a source of livelihood
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the economic aspect of flowers, understanding that they are sold and bought, and how florists manage their business.

subtopics.name: Transformation of flowers into fruits
subtopics.summary: Students observe and understand how flowers change into fruits, identifying the sequential stages from flower to fruit using provided images of a pomegranate.

subtopics.name: Observation of fruit formation and insect interaction
subtopics.summary: Students are encouraged to observe the transformation of flowers into fruits in various plants like guava, ridge gourd, bitter gourd, and beans. They also learn about the role of insects (honeybees, butterflies, beetles) in feeding on nectar from flowers and their contribution to the transformation process.

subtopics.name: Introduction to seeds and germination
subtopics.summary: Students learn that seeds are essential for new plants to grow and are encouraged to think about where seeds come from and how they germinate.

subtopics.name: Identifying seeds in fruits and vegetables
subtopics.summary: Students observe pictures of various fruits and vegetables (guava, bitter gourd, apple, peas, watermelon, pomegranate, grapes, custard apple, mango) and discuss the presence, number, and types of seeds they contain.

subtopics.name: Diversity in seed characteristics
subtopics.summary: Students learn that seeds differ in size, shape, and texture, with examples like small banyan seeds and large coconut seeds, hard-shelled castor seeds, and soft sesame seeds. They also learn about fruits with single seeds (mango, coconut) versus many seeds (guava, pomegranate).

subtopics.name: Understanding seed germination
subtopics.summary: Students learn what germination is, how seeds germinate, and the time it takes. They are introduced to the essential conditions for germination: air, water, and temperature.

subtopics.name: Factors affecting seed germination
subtopics.summary: Students learn that not all seeds germinate and discuss reasons why, such as seed viability or environmental factors. They are prompted to think about what is required for germination and which seeds germinate faster.

subtopics.name: Role of soil organisms in plant growth
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the process of seed germination and the importance of microorganisms (decomposers) and earthworms in making the soil fertile, thus supporting plant growth.

subtopics.name: Methods of seed dispersal
subtopics.summary: Students learn that seeds are dispersed by wind (e.g., Calotropis/Zilledu), water, animals (e.g., Caltrops/Palleru, Tiger's claw/Telukondikaya), and human beings, allowing plants to grow in new locations.

subtopics.name: Understanding plant nurseries
subtopics.summary: Students learn what a plant nursery is, where to find different types of plants (flowering, fruit-bearing), and the role of government departments like the Forest Department in growing and supplying plants.

subtopics.name: Plant propagation methods and biodiversity
subtopics.summary: Students are introduced to various methods of plant propagation used in nurseries, such as grafting, tissue culture, and sprinkling seeds. They also learn about the importance of nurseries in growing diverse varieties of plants and their role in environmental protection and preventing pollution.

subtopics.name: Benefits of planting trees
subtopics.summary: Students discuss the benefits of trees for human beings, animals, and birds, and are encouraged to participate in plantation programs like "clean and green" in their schools and homes.

subtopics.name: Review of plant concepts and skills
subtopics.summary: Students review key concepts related to plants, including plant types, parts, uses, flowers as livelihood, nurseries, and conditions for seed germination. They also engage in activities to develop questioning, experimental, information, expression, and appreciation skills related to plants and biodiversity.

subtopics.name: Understanding village environment
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the components of a village such as families, houses, and natural surroundings like mountains, lakes, and fields.

subtopics.name: Identifying cardinal directions using the sun
subtopics.summary: Students learn that the sun rises in the East, and by facing the sun, they can determine East, West (behind), South (right hand), and North (left hand).

subtopics.name: Relative positioning and directions
subtopics.summary: Students learn to determine the direction of various objects (well, bus-stop, school, mango tree) from a given reference point (Kasturi).

subtopics.name: Understanding boundaries and location
subtopics.summary: Students learn that directions help in identifying boundaries and locating places like houses, streets, and buildings.

subtopics.name: Understanding cardinal and intercardinal directions
subtopics.summary: Students are introduced to the eight main directions, including North, South, East, West, and the intermediate directions like North West, North East, South West, and South East.

subtopics.name: Identifying corners
subtopics.summary: Students learn that the space or point between two directions is called a corner and are asked to identify corners in their surroundings.

subtopics.name: Introduction to map drawing
subtopics.summary: Students learn that maps are drawings of large areas on paper showing directions and scale.

subtopics.name: 6.3.1. Measurements of the classroom
subtopics.summary: Students learn how to measure the dimensions of a classroom using a non-standard unit (match sticks) to understand the concept of scale in map drawing.

subtopics.name: Applying scale to measure and draw a classroom map
subtopics.summary: Students apply the concept of scale to measure the classroom walls and then draw a map of the classroom, understanding how to represent larger distances in a smaller format.

subtopics.name: Identifying features and boundaries of a village
subtopics.summary: Students learn how directions and corners help identify places and boundaries within a village, such as roads, buildings, and natural features.

subtopics.name: Interpreting a village map
subtopics.summary: Students learn to read and understand a symbolic map of a village, identifying different structures and natural elements.

subtopics.name: Definition and components of a Mandal
subtopics.summary: Students learn that a Mandal is an administrative unit comprising several villages and houses various public institutions like government offices and health centers.

subtopics.name: Introduction to Mandal maps
subtopics.summary: Students are informed that maps can also be made for larger administrative units like Mandals, similar to village maps.

subtopics.name: Interpreting a Mandal map
subtopics.summary: Students learn to read and analyze a map showing different villages within a Mandal and identify their directional relationships and geographical features like rivers.

subtopics.name: Understanding a District
subtopics.summary: Students learn that many villages make a mandal, and many mandals make a district. They are introduced to key district institutions like the Collectorate Office, Zilla Parishad Office, district hospital, and other public institutions. The text mentions the number of districts in the state and that each district has its own map.

subtopics.name: Observing a District Map
subtopics.summary: Students observe a given map of Nalgonda District, identifying its boundaries and the mandals within it.

subtopics.name: Understanding a State
subtopics.summary: Students learn that some districts together form a state. The text introduces Telangana as their state, mentions its 33 districts, Hyderabad as its capital, and highlights its diverse culture, festivals, rivers (Godavari, Krishna, Tungabhadra, Musi), and crops (paddy, jowar, maize, sugarcane).

subtopics.name: Observing State and India Maps
subtopics.summary: Students observe a small map of India and a detailed map of Telangana, identifying districts and boundaries.

subtopics.name: Vocabulary List
subtopics.summary: A list of ten key words: map, village, mandal, district, state, country, boundaries (limits), directions (sides), corners, Public institutions.

subtopics.name: Conceptual Understanding
subtopics.summary: Students answer questions about directions relative to their house and school, and interpret a simple map showing locations around a person named Chandu.

subtopics.name: Questioning, Hypotheses, Experiments & Field Observations
subtopics.summary: Students are prompted to formulate questions based on a map, draw maps of their classroom, and conduct field observations of nearby houses to compare their layouts and features.

subtopics.name: Information Skills & Projects
subtopics.summary: Students engage in a project to visit houses, record observations in a table, and discuss patterns and relationships between house arrangements and directions.

subtopics.name: Communication through Mapping Skills, Drawing Pictures and Making Models
subtopics.summary: Students practice various mapping and model-making skills, including preparing a school model, drawing maps of their house, village/city, mandal, and Telangana, and identifying geographical features.

subtopics.name: Appreciation, Values and Awareness towards Bio-diversity
subtopics.summary: Students reflect on the uses and importance of maps and the information they provide, including aspects related to biodiversity.

subtopics.name: Self-Assessment: I can do these
subtopics.summary: A checklist for students to self-assess their ability to speak about directions, ask questions about maps, prepare models, and use maps for information.

subtopics.name: Introduction to Public Institutions
subtopics.summary: Describes a village facing a flood and the problems that arise, leading to the need for public institutions.

subtopics.name: 7.1. Public Institutions - Gram Panchayat
subtopics.summary: Explains the role of Gram Panchayat in providing services, illustrated by Mohan's story of applying for a ration card and interacting with Tahsildar and District Civil Supplies Officer.

subtopics.name: Details of the functions
subtopics.summary: Lists various functions performed by public servants related to village administration, such as crop details, house tax, street lights, land records, births/deaths, revenue work, water bills, letters, school maintenance, drains, and vaccination.

subtopics.name: Fair Price Shops
subtopics.summary: Explains that the government provides provisions to the poor through Fair Price Shops and encourages students to visit one to collect information.

subtopics.name: 7.2. Public Institutions - Mandal Headquarters
subtopics.summary: Introduces the Tahsildar office, Mandal Revenue Office, and their functions related to Ration Cards and Land Revenue.

subtopics.name: Mandal Parishad Office
subtopics.summary: Describes the activities of the Mandal Parishad Office, including primary education, agriculture, construction, health, law & order, crime reduction, public relations, enquiries, people's welfare, and child protection.

subtopics.name: Police Station
subtopics.summary: Describes the activities of the Police Station, including maintaining law & order, reducing crime, receiving complaints, conducting enquiries, helping people, and safeguarding child-related issues.

subtopics.name: Mandal Revenue Office
subtopics.summary: Describes the activities of the Mandal Revenue Office, including supervision of VROs, handling complaints, ensuring government programmes, issuing certificates, relieving bonded labour, sanctioning Land Holder Pass books, and resolving land issues.

subtopics.name: Primary Health Centre
subtopics.summary: Describes the activities of the Primary Health Centre, including treating common diseases, supervising health sub-centres, implementing national health programmes, ensuring safe deliveries, and referring needy cases.

subtopics.name: Bank
subtopics.summary: Describes the activities of a bank (SBI), including accepting deposits, distributing loans, collecting daily deposits, distributing loans to Women Self Help Groups, and providing ATM services.

subtopics.name: Mandal Resource Centre
subtopics.summary: Describes the activities of the Mandal Resource Centre, including school enrolment, monitoring schools, supervising schools, addressing irregular teachers, implementing mid-day meal programmes, and distributing free textbooks and uniforms.

subtopics.name: Veterinary Hospital
subtopics.summary: Describes the activities of the Veterinary Hospital, including animal health care, diagnosis and treatment, identifying diseases, providing suggestions for animal rearing, and educating farmers on animal nutrition.

subtopics.name: Mandal Level Meetings and Leadership
subtopics.summary: Explains the process of Mandal level meetings, the roles of Sarpanch and Mandal President, and includes an example of an invitation letter for a General Body Meeting.

subtopics.name: Introduction to Mandal General Body Meeting Agenda
subtopics.summary: Students are introduced to the concept of an 'agenda' for a general body meeting at the Mandal level.

subtopics.name: Key Agenda Points for Mandal Meetings
subtopics.summary: Students identify various topics discussed in a Mandal general body meeting, such as agriculture, animal husbandry, education, health, and infrastructure.

subtopics.name: Roles of Mandal Level Officials
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the roles of different Mandal level officials, including the Mandal Parishad Development Officer, Tahsildar, Agricultural Officer, Veterinary Officer, Mandal Educational Officer, and Health Officer, in addressing community issues.

subtopics.name: Common Issues Discussed at Mandal Meetings
subtopics.summary: Students review a list of common questions and problems discussed at Mandal meetings, covering areas like agriculture, infrastructure, sanitation, and public services.

subtopics.name: Introduction to District Level Institutions
subtopics.summary: Students are introduced to the concept of public institutions operating at the district level, such as the Collectorate.

subtopics.name: Case Study: Applying for Arogyasree Card
subtopics.summary: Through a story about Kiran's illness, students learn about the process of applying for an Arogyasree card and the role of the District Collector in such applications.

subtopics.name: Role of the District Collector
subtopics.summary: Students understand that the District Collector is a key authority at the district level, responsible for various administrative functions, including processing applications for welfare schemes like Arogyasree.

subtopics.name: Other District Level Government Departments
subtopics.summary: Students learn that besides the Mandal Office, there are numerous other government departments at the district level that serve the public.

subtopics.name: Definition and Examples of Public Property
subtopics.summary: Students understand that public institutions and resources like street lights, water pumps, and hospitals are public property, funded by taxes, and belong to everyone.

subtopics.name: Importance of Protecting Public Property
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the responsibility of citizens to protect public property to ensure progress and prevent loss, recognizing that damage to public assets affects everyone.

subtopics.name: Introduction to Right to Information
subtopics.summary: Students are introduced to the concept of the Right to Information through a dialogue about a community problem and the process of seeking information from a public office.

subtopics.name: Understanding the RTI Act - 2005
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the key provisions of the Right to Information Act, 2005, including the right to access government records, the application process, fees, and response timelines.

subtopics.name: Process of Applying for Information
subtopics.summary: Students are shown an example of an application form for information and learn the steps involved in requesting information under the RTI Act, including necessary details and enclosures.

subtopics.name: Dialogue on Using RTI
subtopics.summary: Students follow a dialogue that illustrates how citizens can use the Right to Information Act to address local problems and obtain information from public institutions.

subtopics.name: Key Vocabulary
subtopics.summary: Students review and learn important terms related to public institutions, such as Gram Panchayat, Mandal Parishad, Collectorate, Arogyasree card, and public property.

subtopics.name: Conceptual Understanding
subtopics.summary: Students answer questions to demonstrate their understanding of public institutions, their functions, and related concepts like the Right to Information Act.

subtopics.name: Questioning and Hypotheses
subtopics.summary: Students engage in an activity to identify district-level offices and formulate questions to understand the benefits they provide to the public.

subtopics.name: Field Observations
subtopics.summary: Students are encouraged to visit a local public institution and document its functions, fostering practical understanding.

subtopics.name: Observing different kinds of houses
subtopics.summary: Students observe various types of houses shown in pictures, such as huts, mud houses, tiled houses, modern houses, and apartment buildings.

subtopics.name: Traditional house construction and materials
subtopics.summary: Students learn about how houses were constructed in the past, using materials like mud, bamboo sticks, wooden logs, and cow dung, and the reasons for these choices.

subtopics.name: Modern house construction and materials
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the materials and techniques used in modern house construction, including bricks, cement, concrete, steel, plastering, and various types of flooring and roofing.

subtopics.name: Duplex houses and their features
subtopics.summary: Students are introduced to the concept of a duplex house, its construction process, and the different rooms and areas within it, often with the help of a floor plan.

subtopics.name: Roof gardens
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the concept of a roof garden, its purpose, and how it is created on a house.

subtopics.name: Stages of brick making
subtopics.summary: Students learn the sequential stages involved in manufacturing bricks, including collecting clay, mixing with ash and water, moulding, drying, burning in a kiln, and cooling.

subtopics.name: Types of stones used in construction
subtopics.summary: Students learn about different types of stones used for foundation and flooring, such as Tandur stones, Rajasthan Marble, Shabad (Granite) stones, and Khammam granite.

subtopics.name: Gravel and stone crushing
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the use of gravel in concrete preparation and the process of crushing huge rocks into smaller pieces using machines, including the living conditions of the workers involved.

subtopics.name: Reasons and characteristics of apartment construction
subtopics.summary: Students learn why apartments are built in towns and cities (due to high land prices), how they accommodate many families in 'flats', and their increasing size over time.

subtopics.name: Tools and machines for apartment construction
subtopics.summary: Students identify and learn about specific tools and machines used in constructing apartments, such as concrete mixers, cranes, and lifts for materials.

subtopics.name: Regional factors influencing house construction
subtopics.summary: Students learn that house construction varies based on local climate, temperature, rainfall, and available materials.

subtopics.name: Houses in North-eastern regions
subtopics.summary: Students learn about houses in damp North-eastern regions (Assam, Meghalaya, Nagaland) which are often constructed with wood, bamboo, clay, cow dung, slanting roofs, and built on stilts to protect against dampness and allow water drainage.

subtopics.name: Houses in Kashmir
subtopics.summary: Students learn about houses in Kashmir, a cool region, including houseboats called 'Donga' found on Dal Lake.

subtopics.name: Housing challenges for different income groups
subtopics.summary: Students learn that many people live in rented or temporary houses, and poor people often do not own their own houses.

subtopics.name: Government's role in providing housing
subtopics.summary: Students learn that the government constructs houses for those who cannot afford them.

subtopics.name: Slums and living conditions
subtopics.summary: Students observe and learn about the living conditions in slums, where poor people live in huts beside streams, river banks, and open places.

subtopics.name: Practice of open defecation
subtopics.summary: Students learn that many rural villages lack toilet facilities, leading people to excrete in open places.

subtopics.name: Health consequences of open defecation
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the health problems caused by open defecation, such as flies spreading harmful bacteria, contamination of water leading to diseases like cholera and typhoid, and worms causing anaemia.

subtopics.name: Government efforts for sanitation
subtopics.summary: Students learn that the government is working to provide toilet facilities to all and offers financial aid to build toilets.

subtopics.name: Anitabai's story: A case study in demanding sanitation
subtopics.summary: Students read the story of Anitabai, who demanded a toilet in her village and received a cash award from the President for her initiative, highlighting the importance of individual action in promoting sanitation.

subtopics.name: Importance of sanitation for health
subtopics.summary: Students learn that "Health is wealth" and unhygienic, dirty surroundings are major causes of diseases, making sanitation a compulsory requirement.

subtopics.name: Garbage observation and types
subtopics.summary: Students are encouraged to observe garbage collected at their houses and learn to differentiate between wet trash (food leaves, peels, fruits) and dry trash (covers, papers).

subtopics.name: Composting and segregation of garbage
subtopics.summary: Students learn about making compost pits for wet trash and the importance of separating wet and dry garbage for municipal collection.

subtopics.name: Environmental impact of burning garbage
subtopics.summary: Students learn that burning garbage is harmful to the environment as it pollutes the air.

subtopics.name: Three principles for environmental protection: Reduce, Re-use, Re-cycling
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the three key principles to protect the environment: reducing waste (e.g., avoiding plastic), re-using items (e.g., bags, covers), and re-cycling materials (e.g., iron, plastic, glass, paper).

subtopics.name: Characteristics of a healthy living environment
subtopics.summary: Students learn that a good house should have sufficient light, free air, and plants in its surroundings to be pleasant and healthy.

subtopics.name: Key words
subtopics.summary: Students review and learn important vocabulary related to houses, construction, and sanitation, including terms like mason, apartment, roof garden, tiled house, mud slab, brick kiln, building, house roof, stone crusher, house plan, slums, cement concrete, and sanitation.

subtopics.name: The Importance of Nagulacheruvu Tank
subtopics.summary: Students learn about Nagulacheruvu as a main source of water for agriculture and the impact of rainfall on crops, through a narrative from Varalakshmi.

subtopics.name: Impact of Flood Water and Sludge
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the effects of flood water causing tanks to overflow and fill with sludge, and the general knowledge about tanks in surrounding villages.

subtopics.name: 9.1. History of Nagulacheruvu
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the historical context of Nagulacheruvu, its agricultural significance, and the contributions of individuals like Mallikarjun and Abdulbari in its development and maintenance.

subtopics.name: 9.2. Construction of a tank
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the collective effort involved in tank construction, the source of flood water, and the methods used to build bunds and outlets.

subtopics.name: Details of Outlet and Sluice Construction
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the technical aspects of constructing outlets and sluices, their function in regulating water flow, preventing soil erosion, and the historical context of tank construction by dynasties.

subtopics.name: 9.3. Big tanks of our state
subtopics.summary: An introduction to the concept of large tanks within the state.

subtopics.name: 9.3.1. Ramappa tank:
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the Ramappa tank, its location near the Ramappa temple, its historical construction, and its role as a water source for agriculture.

subtopics.name: 9.3.2. Panagal tank:
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the Panagal tank, its location in Nalgonda district, its construction by the Kakatiya King Prathaparudra, and its use for irrigation.

subtopics.name: 9.3.3. Hussain Sagar
subtopics.summary: Students learn about Hussain Sagar, its location in Hyderabad, its historical construction, its bund connecting twin cities, and issues like pollution and silting.

subtopics.name: Osman Sagar
subtopics.summary: Students learn about Osman Sagar, its location, construction on the Musi river, and its importance as a source of fresh drinking water for Hyderabad.

subtopics.name: 9.4. Relation between the farmers and the tanks
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the deep connection between farmers and tanks, illustrated through Chennaiah's story, covering activities like washing, fishing, and cultural practices.

subtopics.name: 9.5. Advantages of Tanks
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the various benefits provided by tanks, including water for agriculture, daily chores, breeding fish, and supporting the growth of fibre plants.

subtopics.name: Water Sources and Tank Maintenance
subtopics.summary: Students learn about borewells and water tanks as supplementary water sources, the effects of decreasing water levels, and the practice of desiltation to increase tank capacity.

subtopics.name: 9.6. Tanks-Pollution
subtopics.summary: Students are introduced to the concept of tank pollution and encouraged to observe visual examples of activities that contribute to it.

subtopics.name: Specific Causes of Tank Pollution
subtopics.summary: Students learn about various sources of tank pollution including washing clothes, bathing cattle, vehicle washing, idol immersion, sewage discharge, littering, and industrial chemical effluents.

subtopics.name: Consequences of Tank Pollution
subtopics.summary: Students understand the negative impacts of polluted tank water, such as making it unfit for drinking and causing fish to die.

subtopics.name: Community Action Against Pollution
subtopics.summary: Students learn about instances where villagers collectively opposed the establishment of industries near tanks and are prompted to reflect on their own experiences with collective action against harmful activities.

subtopics.name: Cultural and Economic Importance of Tanks
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the role of tanks in daily life, during harvest season, and for festivals like Bathukamma, highlighting their importance to the community.

subtopics.name: Biodiversity Supported by Tanks
subtopics.summary: Students are introduced to the various forms of life found around tanks, including birds, insects, fish, lotus, and water lilies, emphasizing the ecological value of tanks.

subtopics.name: Drying of Tanks - Drought
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the phenomenon of tanks drying up and its causes, such as environmental pollution, deforestation, and reduction in greenery.

subtopics.name: Detailed Causes of Tank Drying and Drought
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the broader causes of drought and tank drying, including increased sunrays, decreasing rainfall, drilling of borewells, and depletion of groundwater.

subtopics.name: Role of Agricultural Water Management Societies (AWMS) in Tank Maintenance
subtopics.summary: Students are introduced to the concept of AWMS and their activities in maintaining tanks, such as repairs, desiltation, and managing sluices and outlets.

subtopics.name: Government Initiatives for Tank Conservation
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the "Mission Kakatiya" program by the Telangana government, aimed at desiltation to increase the water storage capacity of tanks.

subtopics.name: Udaipur - The city of lakes
subtopics.summary: Students learn about Udaipur, a city in Rajasthan known for its many lakes, including historical context and important lakes like Pichola, Jagnivas, and City Palace.

subtopics.name: Key Vocabulary
subtopics.summary: Students are presented with a list of 15 key words related to tanks, water management, and agriculture, such as 'agriculture', 'canal', 'tanks', 'pollution of tanks', and 'tank management'.

subtopics.name: Conceptual Understanding
subtopics.summary: Students answer questions to demonstrate their understanding of why tanks are needed, their purposes, the role of AWMS, crop selection, uses of outlets, farmers' dependence on tanks, and tank conservation.

subtopics.name: Questioning and Hypotheses
subtopics.summary: Students engage in a scenario-based question to practice forming hypotheses and asking relevant questions about tanks.

subtopics.name: Field Observations and Experiments
subtopics.summary: Students are instructed to visit a nearby tank and field to observe and record their findings, promoting hands-on learning and direct engagement with the environment.

subtopics.name: Information Skills and Projects
subtopics.summary: Students undertake a research project to write about a famous tank, draw its diagram, and use various resources like books, magazines, and the internet for information.

subtopics.name: Communication through Mapping, Drawing, and Models
subtopics.summary: Students practice geographical and artistic skills by collecting names of tanks, identifying their locations on a Telangana map, and potentially drawing pictures or making models.

subtopics.name: Appreciation, Values and Awareness towards Bio-diversity
subtopics.summary: Students engage with questions that foster an appreciation for the value of tanks as an "elixir of life," promote participation in conservation efforts, and raise awareness about the impact of human activities on tanks and biodiversity.

subtopics.name: Self-Assessment of Learning
subtopics.summary: Students use a checklist to assess their ability to explain tank construction, ask questions about tanks, identify important tanks, exhibit tank history, and write slogans for protection.

subtopics.name: Example of a Major Tank
subtopics.summary: Students are shown an image of "The Laknavaram Cheruvu, Warangal" as an example of an important tank.

subtopics.name: Introduction to Food and Health
subtopics.summary: Students learn why food is needed, that food habits vary, and that food comes from plants and animals.

subtopics.name: Picnic Story
subtopics.summary: Students read a narrative about a family picnic, including food preparation and activities.

subtopics.name: Understanding Mid Day Meals
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the mid-day meal program, its purpose, and the importance of nutritional food for children.

subtopics.name: Food Spoilage and Health
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the disadvantages of wasting food, what happens if stale food is eaten, how food gets contaminated, and the importance of proper care to keep food safe.

subtopics.name: Good Food Habits
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the importance of eating fresh, hot, and covered food, and personal hygiene habits related to food.

subtopics.name: Microorganisms and Food Spoilage
subtopics.summary: Students learn about microorganisms spoiling food and conduct simple experiments to observe food spoilage.

subtopics.name: Methods of Food Preservation
subtopics.summary: Students learn why food needs to be preserved and common methods like using salt, drying, and making pickles, chutneys, jams.

subtopics.name: Janata Fridge Construction and Comparison
subtopics.summary: Students learn how to construct a Janata Fridge using earthen pots and understand its benefits. They compare it with an electrical refrigerator in terms of cost, availability, and environmental impact.

subtopics.name: Food wastage on occasions
subtopics.summary: Discusses how food is wasted during weddings, parties, and functions.

subtopics.name: Environmental impact of plastic plates
subtopics.summary: Explains the harm caused by plastic plates and tumblers to the environment and soil.

subtopics.name: Traditional leaf plates
subtopics.summary: Introduces the use of leaf plates from trees like butea (moduga) and banyan as an eco-friendly alternative.

subtopics.name: Causes of food wastage at home
subtopics.summary: Explains how food is wasted at home, including leftover food and spoiled food grains.

subtopics.name: Impact of pests on stored food
subtopics.summary: Describes how insects, fungi, and rats can damage and spoil stored food grains.

subtopics.name: Making agricultural tools and implements
subtopics.summary: Shows people involved in creating tools for farming.

subtopics.name: Watering crops
subtopics.summary: Depicts the process of irrigating crops.

subtopics.name: Harvesting the crop and storing in bags
subtopics.summary: Illustrates the collection of crops and their storage.

subtopics.name: Selling the crop at the market
subtopics.summary: Shows the process of selling harvested crops.

subtopics.name: Buying food grains at a grocery shop
subtopics.summary: Depicts the act of purchasing food items from a shop.

subtopics.name: Eating food obtained with the efforts and labour of different people
subtopics.summary: Concludes the journey with the consumption of food, acknowledging the labor involved.

subtopics.name: Key vocabulary
subtopics.summary: Lists important terms introduced in the chapter.

subtopics.name: Conceptual understanding
subtopics.summary: Questions designed to check understanding of core concepts.

subtopics.name: Questioning and hypotheses
subtopics.summary: Questions encouraging critical thinking and forming hypotheses.

subtopics.name: Experiments and field observations
subtopics.summary: Activities involving practical observation and experimentation.

subtopics.name: Communication through mapping, drawing, and models
subtopics.summary: Creative activities to express understanding through visual means.

subtopics.name: Appreciation, values, and awareness towards bio-diversity
subtopics.summary: Questions promoting appreciation for efforts in food production and healthy habits.

subtopics.name: Self-assessment
subtopics.summary: A set of statements for students to evaluate their own learning and understanding.

subtopics.name: Ravi Prakash's Prize and Trip Announcement
subtopics.summary: Students read about Ravi Prakash winning a prize for his drawing and the announcement of his trip to Delhi with his uncle.

subtopics.name: 11.1. Travelling By Bus
subtopics.summary: Students learn about Ravi Prakash's bus journey, the conversation about ordinary and express buses, and the general experience of bus travel.

subtopics.name: 11.1.1. Problem of change
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the common issue of needing exact change for bus tickets and the importance of cooperation with the conductor.

subtopics.name: 11.1.2. Traffic signals
subtopics.summary: Students observe and learn about traffic signals, zebra crossings, speed breakers, and the role of traffic police in ensuring road safety, along with a list of rules for riding/driving vehicles.

subtopics.name: 11.2. Train Journey
subtopics.summary: Students learn about Ravi Prakash's arrival at Secunderabad railway station, his first experience with train travel, and the process of checking tickets and boarding the train.

subtopics.name: 11.2.1. Types of Clothes - Languages
subtopics.summary: Students observe the diversity of people in the train, noting different types of dresses and languages spoken, and how railway announcements and instructions reflect this diversity.

subtopics.name: 11.2.2. Food in the train
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the food service on trains, where food is prepared, and the facilities available like toilets and wash basins.

subtopics.name: 11.2.3. Cleanliness in Trains
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the importance of cleanliness on trains, proper waste disposal, and hygiene practices during travel.

subtopics.name: 11.2.4. Railway signals
subtopics.summary: Students learn about railway signals, their purpose, and the operation of level crossings, including safety precautions.

subtopics.name: Conclusion of Journey and Key Vocabulary
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the conclusion of Ravi Prakash's journey to Delhi, his return by plane, and review key terms related to travel.

subtopics.name: Conceptual Understanding
subtopics.summary: Students answer questions to demonstrate their understanding of travel precautions, bus rules, railway safety, differences between bus and train journeys, and reasons for travel preferences.

subtopics.name: Questioning - Hypotheses
subtopics.summary: Students are encouraged to formulate questions based on observations at railway stations and bus stands, specifically about travel from their village to Delhi.

subtopics.name: Experiments - Field Observations
subtopics.summary: Students are instructed to visit a bus stand or railway station to observe and document details such as station names, available facilities, and arrival/departure boards.

subtopics.name: Information Skills, Projects
subtopics.summary: Students are tasked with collecting information about trains from newspapers and filling out a railway reservation form.

subtopics.name: Expression of ideas through Mapping Skills, Drawing Pictures and Making Models
subtopics.summary: Students engage in activities such as identifying railway junctions on a map, creating models of transport, and discussing travel routes on a map of India.

subtopics.name: Appreciation, Values and Awareness towards Bio-diversity
subtopics.summary: Students reflect on travel precautions, responsible passenger behavior, maintaining cleanliness, and helping co-passengers, including senior citizens, women, and children.

subtopics.name: Self-Assessment Statements
subtopics.summary: Students review a list of statements related to journey details, facilities, and responsible behavior, indicating whether they can perform each task.

subtopics.name: Introduction to Ancient History and Human Evolution
subtopics.summary: Students learn that ancient history tells how modern man evolved from primitive man. They are asked to observe a picture showing human evolution.

subtopics.name: Life of Primitive Man
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the lifestyle of primitive man, including their nomadic nature, living in caves and burrows, hunting animals, eating raw flesh, and the discovery of fire for cooking.

subtopics.name: Introduction to Indian History
subtopics.summary: Students learn that India has a rich history and that history helps understand past events and changes in lifestyle. They are encouraged to think about how to learn about history.

subtopics.name: Archaeology and the Indus Valley Civilization
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the role of archaeology in uncovering past civilizations, specifically the Indus (Harappan) Valley Civilization. Details about its discovery, urban planning, agriculture, crafts, metals, trade, and important figures like 'Lord Pashupathi' and 'Mother Goddess' are provided.

subtopics.name: Sources of Indian History
subtopics.summary: Students learn that excavations, monuments, inscriptions, museums, and books are sources of information about Indian history and culture. The role of Historical Museums and the Salarjung Museum in Hyderabad, as well as the tribal museum in Srisailam, are highlighted.

subtopics.name: Aspects of Indian Culture
subtopics.summary: Students learn about different elements of Indian culture such as clothes, food, language, games, festivals, and symbols. It is noted that these aspects are not similar to ours today.

subtopics.name: Cultural Values and Heritage
subtopics.summary: Students learn about cultural aspects like lifestyles, thoughts, daily works, art, literature, entertainment, respecting elders, helping others, showing gratitude, praying, worshipping nature, and protecting biodiversity. It emphasizes that all living creatures are equal and contribute to national pride.

subtopics.name: Indian Cultural Heritage
subtopics.summary: Students learn that India has a special place for recognition and respect in the world due to its wonderful sculpture, music, dance, paintings, puranas, and ithihasas, which are part of its cultural heritage. The importance of protecting this culture for future generations is highlighted.

subtopics.name: Introduction to Indian Monuments
subtopics.summary: Students learn that ancient inscriptions, temples, monuments, and stupas reflect the country's history and culture. They can understand history better by visiting and collecting details about them.

subtopics.name: The Saranath Stupa
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the Saranath Stupa, built by Emperor Ashoka, its dome-shaped construction of bricks and stones, and its location near Varanasi in Uttarpradesh, identifying it as a very ancient monument.

subtopics.name: The Askoka's Pillar at Saranath
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the standing stone pillar made of sand stone found at 'Chunar' near Varanasi, featuring four lions engraved on four sides of the pillar, and its Rajasthani style of sculpture.

subtopics.name: The Telangana Martyr Stupa
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the Telangana Martyr Stupa located in Hyderabad, built in memory of people who died during the 1969 Telangana movement, symbolizing the spirit of the movement.

subtopics.name: The Qutub Minar
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the Qutub Minar in Delhi, its construction by Qutubuddin Aibak and completion by Iltutmish, and its height of 72.5 meters.

subtopics.name: The Red Fort
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the Red Fort in Delhi, its significance as the place where the national flag is hoisted on Independence Day, its construction with red stone, and its Persian and Indian architectural style.

subtopics.name: The Charminar
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the Charminar in Hyderabad, its height of 58 meters, its four minarets, and its construction year of 1591.

subtopics.name: The Thousand Pillars Temple
subtopics.summary: Students learn about the Thousand Pillars Temple in Warangal, dedicated to Lord Shiva, belonging to the Kakatiya period, and its beautiful sculptures.

subtopics.name: The Emperor Ashoka
subtopics.summary: Students learn about Emperor Ashoka, grandson of Chandragupta Maurya, founder of the Mauryan Empire, his distress after the Kalinga war, his adoption of Buddhism, and his promotion of non-violence, with the 'Dharmachakra' being part of the National Flag, and the Saranath Stupa built by him.

subtopics.name: Chandragupta Vikramaditya
subtopics.summary: Students learn about Srig[?]upta establishing the Gupta empire, Chandragupta Vikramaditya being the most popular king, the 'Navaratnas' (nine poets) in his court including Kalidasa, and the famous Ellora caves belonging to the Gupta period with sculptures and paintings.

subtopics.name: Ganapatideva and Rudramadevi
subtopics.summary: Students learn about Ganapatideva and his daughter Rudrama Devi, famous among the Kakatiyas, who united the Telugu speaking people. The Warangal Fort, the Thousand Pillars Temple, the Ramappa Temple, and tanks like Pakala and Ramappa were built during the Kakatiya period.

subtopics.name: Sri Krishna Devaraya
subtopics.summary: Students learn about Sri Krishna Devaraya, the most famous among the Vijayanagara kings (1509 to 1529), his court having eight poets called "Ashtadiggajas", and his work 'Amuktamalyada' written in Telugu, which is considered the best among all languages of the country.

subtopics.name: Akbar
subtopics.summary: Students learn about Akbar, the most famous Mughal Emperor who ruled India, his identity as a Mohammedan, and his tolerance towards other religions, making him a great administrator.

subtopics.name: Shivaji
subtopics.summary: Students learn about Shivaji, a brave man who revolted against the Mughals and established the Maratha Kingdom, his Hinduism, tolerance towards other religions, and his guru Samarth Ramadas, who taught him great saintship.

subtopics.name: Mir Usman Ali Khan
subtopics.summary: Students learn about Mir Usman Ali Khan, the last ruler of Nizams, who ruled Hyderabad, his identity as a Mohammedan, his tolerance towards Hindus, his development of agriculture and industries, and his establishment of educational institutions. They also learn about his major projects like the Nizam Sagar and the Ali Sagar, and the Osman Sagar project for drinking water.

subtopics.name: Key words
subtopics.summary: Students review and learn key vocabulary related to Indian history and culture, including terms like history, culture, primitive man, life style, civilization, country's culture, empire, rulers, and religious tolerance.

subtopics.name: Conceptual Understanding
subtopics.summary: Students answer questions to demonstrate their understanding of the similarities and differences between primitive and modern man, sources of Indian history, the importance of protecting monuments, and significant people and their contributions.

subtopics.name: Questioning - Hypotheses
subtopics.summary: Students engage in critical thinking by formulating questions about historical details when visiting monuments or forts and identifying relevant people to ask.

subtopics.name: Experiments - Field Observations
subtopics.summary: Students are encouraged to visit their village, observe, and explain elements that reveal its history.

subtopics.name: Information Skills, Projects
subtopics.summary: Students practice information organization by preparing a table of important monuments and persons, and collecting and exhibiting details about the Kakatiyas.

subtopics.name: Expression of ideas through Mapping Skills, Drawing Pictures and Making Models
subtopics.summary: Students identify important monuments on an Indian map, identify places belonging to Kakatiyas and Satavahanas in Telangana, and perform mono-action to depict famous personalities.

subtopics.name: Appreciation, Values and Awareness towards Bio-diversity
subtopics.summary: Students reflect on how to protect monuments, express appreciation for their country's culture, identify cultural elements they like, and discuss ways to protect them.

subtopics.name: Can I do these
subtopics.summary: Students self-assess their ability to explain history and culture, ask questions about history, collect details about village history, write about important persons, identify monuments and places, and protect monuments.

subtopics.name: 6. Appreciation - Values, Creating Awareness towards Bio-diversity
subtopics.summary: Students will be able to show kindness towards birds and animals, develop awareness towards environment and bio-diversity, understand measures to protect water, plants and trees, develop good habits related to bio-diversity, understand and implement rules and regulations, practise sportsman spirit, develop appreciable attitudes, and identify and appreciate good in others.

subtopics.name: The learner....
subtopics.summary: The learner will be able to identify simple features of flowers, roots, fruits, birds, and animals; explain family and group relationships; describe different skilled works; differentiate between objects and activities; identify signs, locations, and directions using maps; use information signboards; create collages, designs, models, rangolis, posters, and simple maps; voice opinions on issues; suggest ways of hygiene, reduce, reuse, recycle; observe rules in games; explain history of the country and functions of public institutions.

Right to Information Act - 2005 The Right to Information Act is the right to get the required information from Public Institutions (Govt. Offices). We have a right to know the details of the records, documents, memos, orders, reports of government offices etc. For this, we have to submit the required application to the officer concerned. We can collect the details in written form from the offices concerned. We can write the application on a white paper also. We have to pay a nominal application fee. There is no fee at the village level. The people who are below poverty line need not pay any fee. The officer concerned should give the information within 30 days. If the application is about Right to Live and Freedom of Individual, then the information should be given within 48 hours. Commission for information functions as an autonomous body at the state and the centre level. Public Information Officer is present in every government office. Time taken to provide the information is displayed in the notice boards of the offices. People are a part of the government. Everyone has a right to get the information about the working of an office and other required information. The main aim of the Act is to give the information to the people voluntarily by the government.

6.3.1 Panguri tank
Panguri tank is located near the Panguri river. It is a small river tank built during the 15th century with an area of 154 hectares. This tank is used for drinking water and also for the purpose of fishing. In the past, the people used to throw stones into this tank. Panguri temple is known for its religi- ous significance.
6.3.2 Pangri tank
Pangri tank is also Nagaland's famous it is also called as Udyan Kamalpur. Second it is 6 km away from the Panguri river. It is a famous tourist attraction. It is also known as the 'gateway to the hills'. It is the source of irrigation for thousands of people. It is also a famous hill station. It is located here.

Do you know? Udaipur - The city of lakes Udaipur city in the state of Rajasthan is called the city of Lakes. There are many lakes around Udaipur. Hence the name. In the ancient period, these lakes were constructed to use the water for drinking and agricultural purposes. Among all the lakes in Udaipur, the 'Pichola' lake is an important one. It was built in 1362 by the Banj[?]ras. Later on, it was developed by King Udai Singh. All the places in Udaipur were built on the banks of these lakes. Among them, 'Jagnivas' and 'City Palace' are important.
